description: Standard-scale a CSV and compare K-Means, Agglomerative (ward), Gaussian Mixture, and DBSCAN at n_clusters=N_CLUSTERS, scoring each with silhouette (higher better), Calinski-Harabasz (higher better), and Davies-Bouldin (lower better).
rules: read-only with respect to inputs; writes only under record_store
all numeric columns are used as features; data is standard-scaled first
DBSCAN (eps=0.5, min_samples=5) ignores N_CLUSTERS; it is scored only if it finds >1 cluster, excluding noise points
K-Means and Gaussian Mixture use random_state=42 for reproducibility
graceful: if scikit-learn is absent the porter still writes a valid non-empty JSON note
usage: Set DATA_CSV (numeric feature table). Optional: N_CLUSTERS (default 3). Output: cluster_compare.json.
limitation: Requires scikit-learn + pandas at runtime to produce real scores; offline it degrades to a note. DBSCAN's fixed eps/min_samples may yield a single cluster or all-noise on unscaled-scale-mismatched data.
